task_completion.description: Modify docker-compose.yml to support multiple replicas without conflicts
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing docker-compose.yml being edited to remove static 'container_name' and static host port mappings for the Flask service, and optionally adding 'replicas: 3' under a deploy block.

task_completion.description: Ensure Nginx configuration distributes requests across instances
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the Nginx configuration file being viewed or edited to route traffic to the Flask service's internal DNS name or an upstream block.

task_completion.description: Verify exactly 3 replicas of the Flask API are running
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output of 'docker ps' or 'docker compose ps' showing exactly 3 distinct containers running for the Flask API service, alongside the Nginx container.

task_completion.description: Execute 10 sequential curl requests and save to verification.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing a loop or repeated command (e.g., 'for i in {1..10}; do curl http://localhost:8080 >> verification.txt; done') being executed in the /home/ga/scaling-app/ directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the output file shows requests handled by different hostnames
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing the contents of 'verification.txt' (e.g., using 'cat'), displaying 10 responses with at least two or three different container hostnames/IDs, confirming successful load balancing.

integrity.description: Agent actually executed curl requests against the live server
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual curl commands in the terminal targeting http://localhost:8080. The agent should not use 'echo' commands to fake the output file.

integrity.description: Agent applied the compose changes and started the containers
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for 'docker compose up', 'docker-compose up -d', or 'docker compose up --scale' commands being executed in the terminal, rather than just editing files without applying them.

integrity.description: The verification file contains realistic Docker container hostnames
integrity.visual_evidence: The hostnames in verification.txt should look like genuine short Docker container IDs (e.g., 12-character alphanumeric strings) or standard compose generated names, not manually typed placeholder text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is /home/ga/scaling-app. The expected number of Flask API replicas is exactly 3. The Nginx load balancer must listen on port 8080. The verification file must be saved as /home/ga/scaling-app/verification.txt and should contain the output of 10 curl requests showing different container hostnames handling the traffic.
